Shiloh was located in the territory of Ephraim, the tribe to which Joshua belonged, and it was fitting that the tabernacle should be near the residence of the chief governor. The name of this city is the same as that by which Jacob prophesied of the Messiah. Some suppose that the city was named this way when it was chosen as the resting place for the ark, which symbolized our great Peace-maker and the way to a reconciled God.

After a year or more, Joshua blamed their lack of effort and told them how to proceed. God, by his grace, has given us a claim to a good land, the heavenly Canaan, but we are slow to take possession of it; we do not enter into that rest as we might through faith, hope, and holy joy. How long will this continue with us? How long will we stand in our own way and forsake our own blessings for empty pleasures? Joshua encourages the Israelites to take possession of their territories. He is ready to do his part if they will do theirs.

The boundaries of each portion were clearly defined, and the inheritance of each tribe was settled. All disputes and selfish claims were prevented by God’s wise appointment, who assigned the hills and valleys, the fields of grain and pastureland, the streams and rivers, the towns and cities. Is any servant of Christ’s lot cast in affliction and sorrow? It is the Lord; let him do what seems good to him. Are we in prosperity and peace? It comes from above. Be humbled when you compare the gift with your own unworthiness. Do not forget Him who gave the good, and always be ready to relinquish it at his command.
